content.visible_text: Distance-learning resources Adult Program Forms curriculum The following forms lessons were filmed during an online Forms Seminar  on May 23, 2020. These distance-learning resources were developed during the Shelter-in-Place ordinances of the 2020 Covid-19 pandemic. They don’t function as replacements to our in-person classes, but remain available for our students who would like to do supplemental martial arts at home. To view the written steps to each form, short videos of each form as it is to be performed, and the techniques learned at each rank, please click through the Adult Curriculum page, choosing the belt your student is * currently * wearing. * Below the forms lessons are a collection of hour-long classes that begin with a 30 minute warmups/stretching, and include a 30 minute martial arts lesson. These videos are intended to support students who cannot make it to the regularly-scheduled daily Zoom classes, or would like extra practice. Basic Form: White Belt Gravity: High White Belt Terra Moto: Yellow Belt Riverbed: High Yellow Belt Attitude: Green Belt Waterfall: High Green Spirit: Blue Belt Walk in the Park: High Blue While the Quantum San Francisco Dojo physical location has been closed to promote social distancing during the COVID-19 pandemic, we’ve recorded our Zoom classes and will share a few for the community to practice at home, in the case that you cannot make it to the live classes or would like to take the class again. Lesson Plan: Asterisk Footwork Lesson Plan: Basic Footwork Reviews Facebook Community Dojo Quantum Martial Arts is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit in the State of California, and we thrive on the generosity of people like you. All donations are tax-deductible. Quantum’s tax ID number is 26-1534597. No donation is too small, or too large. Whether it’s $20 or $20,000, every cent will be hugely appreciated and put to good use. Support Our Kids Donate now to support our youth scholarship program which allows families in need to train at reduced cost. We believe investment in our community involves investing in our youth. Our Sister Schools Our Seattle Dojo has served thousands of students since opening on Capitol Hill in 1995, and currently offers youth and adult classes in the Central District. Our Portland Dojo was established in 2020, and currently offers youth and adult classes in Southeast Portland. A WordPress.com Website .
